name: PAPILLARY NECROSIS
definition: NECROTIZING PAPILLITIS ischemic coagulative necrosis of renal papilla (loops of Henle + vasa recta) ← interstitial
dominant_finding: multiple round / triangular cystic spaces in medulla with echo reflections of arcuate arteries
distribution: central necrosis at tip of pyramid
discriminating_features: central necrosis at tip of pyramid
detachment of papilla begins in caliceal fornix
normal or small kidney ← analgesic nephropathy
smooth / wavy renal contour ← analgesic nephropathy)

general: central necrosis at tip of pyramid
detachment of papilla begins in caliceal fornix
normal or small kidney ← analgesic nephropathy
smooth / wavy renal contour ← analgesic nephropathy)
calcification of necrotic papilla: papillary / curvilinear / ringlike
multiple small collections of contrast material in papilla:
"lobster claw" sign = subtle streak of contrast material extending from fornix parallel to
centric / eccentric, thin and short / bulbous cavitation of papilla
displaced collecting system ← enlarged septal cortex ← edema
widened fornix ← necrotic shrinkage of papilla
"signet ring" sign = ring shadow of papilla → outlining detached papilla within contrast
intraluminal nonopaque filling defect in calyx / pelvis / ureter ← expelled sloughed
diminished density of contrast material in nephrogram; rarely increasingly dense
US: multiple round / triangular cystic spaces in medulla with echo reflections of arcuate arteries

clinical.Cx: acute ureteral obstruction after sloughing of papilla; higher incidence of transitional cell
